Surface Logo Turtle

Single-file HTML version. No React, no build step. Run the same Logo-like program on a plane, sphere, torus, cylinder, or cone.
Visible viewsdouble-click to focus one
Surface parameters
Torus ratio2.9
Cylinder ratio2.2
Cone angle50°
Cylinder edge behavior
Cone forward mode
Geodesic follows a straight line on the cone's unwrapped sector. Bearing keeps the same local bearing relative to guide curves.
In geodesic mode, the cone is treated as if you cut it open into a flat sector, drew a straight line there, and wrapped that line back onto the cone. So one long forward move does not usually stay on a guide circle. Bearing mode does.
Run speed10 cmds/frame
Higher values run multiple commands per frame.
Preview size720 px
Shape transparency50%
Downloaded surface-logo-interactive.html.
Commands: FD n forward n units along the current surface FORWARD n full name for FD BK n backward n units BACK n full name for BK RT n turn right in the local tangent plane RIGHT n full name for RT LT n turn left in the local tangent plane LEFT n full name for LT PU pen up PENUP full name for PU PD pen down PENDOWN full name for PD HT hide turtle HIDETURTLE full name for HT ST show turtle SHOWTURTLE full name for ST HOME go to the default starting point CS clear drawing CLEARSCREEN full name for CS SETCOLOR c set stroke color from a palette index, name, or #hex value SETCOLOR r g b set stroke color from RGB values SETWIDTH n set stroke width SETOPACITY n set stroke opacity from 0 to 100 REPEAT n [ ] repeat a block IF cond [ ] run a block when a condition is true Notes: RGB values use mod 256 if outside 0..255. Opacity uses mod 101 if outside 0..100. Comparisons supported in IF: < > <= >= = <> != Procedures: TO name :a :b ... commands END Procedure parameters are referenced as :NAME. Numeric expressions: REPCOUNT current loop index, starting at 1 RANDOM n random integer from 0 to n-1 :NAME procedure parameter reference + - * / arithmetic operators ( ... ) grouping Views: Plane ordinary 2D Logo turtle Sphere FD follows great-circle motion Torus FD follows the current tangent direction on the torus surface Cylinder FD follows the current tangent direction on the cylinder surface Cone FD can use geodesic or local-bearing motion Cone modes: Geodesic straight line on the cone's unwrapped sector Bearing keeps the same local bearing relative to guide curves Tips: Click a shape button to toggle it on or off. Recursive procedure calls are capped at 2048 nested calls to avoid runaway programs locking the page. Double-click a shape button to focus on only that shape. Shape toggles do not recompute immediately; cached views are reused. Examples: REPEAT 12 [ FD 10 * REPCOUNT RT 170 ] REPEAT 16 [ SETCOLOR REPCOUNT FD 8 RT 22 ] REPEAT 10 [ SETCOLOR 255 40 0 SETOPACITY 70 FD 12 RT 144 ] IF 3 > 2 [ FD 20 RT 90 ] TO ARC :SIZE :DEGREES IF :DEGREES < 0 [ REPEAT (0 - :DEGREES) [ FD :SIZE LT 1 ] ] IF :DEGREES >= 0 [ REPEAT :DEGREES [ FD :SIZE RT 1 ] ] END ARC 1 (90 * -1) TO BOX :N REPEAT 4 [ FD :N RT 90 ] END BOX 20

Inspired by Turtle Geometry by Harold Abelson and Andrea diSessa. The same procedure can behave very differently on a plane, sphere, torus, cylinder, or cone.
